what_it_does: Records metadata for a channel attachment that links a board item to the dragnet-development channel.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, with no body content
Target is a board:// URI pointing to item-930addd9
Added timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone offset
standouts: Attachment record is timestamped but carries no payload
conventions: Id prefix att- followed by a hash
board:// URI scheme for cross-system target reference
concerns: No attachment payload or context beyond the target URI
Resolvability and meaning of board://item-930addd9 are not self-contained
notes: A minimal channel-memory attachment entry that points to a board item but contains no attached content.
questions: What content or object does board://item-930addd9 represent?
Is a payload expected for this attachment_type?
Who or what process added this attachment?
intention_signals: Register a board item as an attachment in the dragnet-development channel memory so it can be looked up by channel and target.
novelty: Uses a board:// URI as the sole target pointer with no embedded attachment payload.
contribution: Provides a timestamped channel-to-board-item association that a fused system can treat as a back-reference edge.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter channel attachment record linking dragnet-development to board item item-930addd9 with an added timestamp and no body.
intention_for_embedding: To record that a board item is attached to the dragnet-development channel memory.
